title: Slowly changing dimension types
description: How SCD type 1 (overwrite), type 2 (versioned rows), and type 3 (prior-value column) preserve different amounts of history.
body: Use when a dimension attribute changes over time. Type 1 overwrites the value, keeping no history; type 2 adds a new row per change with effective dates and a current flag, preserving full history; type 3 stores a single previous value in an extra column. Do not use type 1 when past facts must reflect the value current at their event time — it rewrites trends. Actions: pick type 2 for full history, type 1 for corrections, type 3 for a single prior comparison. Validation signals include type-2 rows with non-overlapping validity ranges and facts joined to the time-valid version. Risk boundary: choosing type 1 where history matters silently corrupts analysis; if historical accuracy is required, escalate to type 2 before loading.
